stem: The operating system is responsible for file management on a computer.
model_answer: File management lets users and programs create, open, move, rename, copy and delete files. The OS organises files into directories/folders, keeps track of where each file is stored on disk and enforces permissions so that only authorised users can read or change a file.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Describe two things the operating system does as part of file management.

mark_points.text: provides operations to create, open, move, rename, copy or delete files
organises files into a directory/folder structure and/or tracks where each file is stored on disk (or enforces file access permissions)
accepted_alternatives: maintains directory structure / knows where each file is on disk
controls which users can read or write a file
